title: Fix Snowflake OAuth Documentation to Use Uppercase Identifiers
description:
The Snowflake OAuth integration documentation contained case-sensitivity issues. The `CREATE SECURITY INTEGRATION` statement used an uppercase identifier (`DATATOOLS_OAUTH_INTEGRATION`), but the subsequent `SYSTEM$SHOW_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRETS` call used a different casing. Since Snowflake identifiers are case-sensitive, both must match in all caps.

The documentation was updated to ensure both the security integration name and the secrets query use consistent uppercase identifiers throughout the setup instructions.
acceptance_criteria: The CREATE SECURITY INTEGRATION statement uses an all-caps integration name
The SYSTEM$SHOW_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRETS call uses the same all-caps integration name
Both identifiers match exactly to avoid case-sensitivity errors
Documentation is consistent and copy-paste safe
implementation_notes: Documentation-only change. The Snowflake OAuth setup instructions were updated to use consistent uppercase identifiers for the security integration name across all SQL statements.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the fix was deployed to production.
